name: early_session
description: time_ratio = 0.1 (~10% of the session elapsed; ~10:09 ET in a standard 9:30-16:00 session). Under the M3 re-derivation f(t) = 1 - sqrt(1 - t), decay_curve ~= 0.0513 -- i.e., the new curve has consumed only ~5% of its tightening budget by 10:09 ET. The pre-M3 log10(1+9t) heuristic put decay ~0.279 at the same point (~28% of the budget); the post-M3 curve is ~0.23 looser in decay, meaning ~0.23 percentage-points-of-stop wider at 10:09. A wrong impl that left log10 in place would diverge sharply here -- this fixture catches an unchanged formula.
provenance: DERIVED from the closed-form THEORY formula f(t) = 1 - sqrt(1 - t) (Danielsson & Zigrand, 2003, LSE FMG DP-439). NOT captured from compute_time_squeeze_decay output -- D-2 load-bearing per docs/research/m3-provenance/literature-pass.md §3.1.
